steps.assistant_text: Array-append + count-increment. The 8-insn residual was a pure 2-insn schedule swap: target emits `addu t8,a0,t7` (array addr) BEFORE `sw t6,0x3C(a0)` (count store); natural one-statement-per-line C made IDO schedule the count store first. decomp-permuter found the fix in 80 iters: put both stores on ONE source line (the documented one-line-stores schedule lever). Verified IN-TREE against the full-TU build (8/8 words, 0 diffs), defeating the prior 'standalone false match' caveat.

metadata.verification: in-tree exact: 8/8 words, 0 diffs (no relocs); full `make` ROM byte-identical to baserom; cracked by decomp-permuter (one-line-stores schedule lever)
